codeReview.summary: The change correctly removes the prior module-global actor sharing for focus and maximize state, but it does not fully cover the feature contract because reopen freshness is untested and the maximize-state actor cache can retain stale native-window ownership across close/reopen of the same label.

issues.description: createWindowMaximizedActor() still binds each cached actor to a single Window.getCurrent() instance at creation time, and getWindowMaximizedActor() reuses that actor forever by label. When a secondary window is closed and later reopened with the same label, the reopened renderer reuses the old actor from windowActors instead of creating a fresh one, so maximize-state sync can stay attached to the disposed native window and violate the required open-close-reopen freshness contract.

The added tests only prove that different labels get different actors within one process/module load. They do not exercise closing and reopening a window with the same label, nor do they verify that controls/focus/maximize state are refreshed after reopen, so the feature requirements for VAL-CROSS-004 and the reopen portion of VAL-CONTROLS-007 remain uncovered.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The mission library notes module-scope window-handle caching as a pitfall, but it does not document the stricter rule exposed by this review: any per-window cache keyed only by label must also define teardown/recreation semantics for close-reopen cycles, because Tauri commonly reuses labels like main-1 for reopened windows.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Current mission notes mention Module-scope window-handle caching can bind controls to the wrong window in multi-window sessions in .factory/library/windows.md, while this feature introduced windowActors maps in src/flow/windowMaximized.ts and src/flow/windowFocus.ts without any eviction or reopen-freshness contract.

summary: Fail: renderer-local actor isolation is an improvement, but maximize-state ownership can stay stale across close/reopen of the same window label and the new tests never cover that required regression path.
